repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

Applications running inside `:terminal` that use [DEC private mode 2026](https://gist.github.com/christianparpart/d8a62cc1ab659194337d73e399004036) (synchronized output) to batch screen updates get garbled/overlapping rendering. Neovim's TUI already supports mode 2026 as a *consumer* (via `'termsync'`, PR #25871), but the embedded libvterm in `:terminal` does not support it as a *provider* for applications running inside it.

When applications like [Claude Code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) send `\e[?2026h` to begin a synchronized update, libvterm ignores the sequence. The intermediate screen states during the batched update leak through as visual corruption — overlapping text, duplicated menu items, and garbled permission prompts.

## Solution

Add mode 2026 handling to libvterm's DEC private mode state machine and wire it through to `terminal.c`:

- **`vterm_defs.h`**: Add `VTERM_PROP_SYNCOUTPUT` to the `VTermProp` enum
- **`vterm_internal_defs.h`**: Add `synchronized_output` bit to the state mode struct
- **`state.c`**: Handle mode 2026 in `set_dec_mode()` (routes through `settermprop`), `request_dec_mode()` (DECRQM reply), and `settermprop_internal()` (state storage)
- **`terminal.c`**: Add `synchronized_output` flag to `Terminal` struct. When set, `invalidate_terminal()` accumulates damage but defers scheduling the refresh timer. On `VTERM_PROP_SYNCOUTPUT` disable, triggers `invalidate_terminal()` to flush all accumulated updates as a single atomic refresh

The implementation reuses the existing `settermprop` callback mechanism (same pattern as `VTERM_PROP_THEMEUPDATES`), keeping the change minimal and consistent with the codebase.

**35 lines of implementation, 8 functional tests.**

## Test plan

- [x] `test/functional/terminal/synchronized_output_spec.lua` — 8 tests covering:
  - Content sent inside synchronized updates renders correctly
  - Multiple sequential sync cycles work
  - Content before/during/after sync renders correctly
  - Mode 2026 set/reset does not crash
  - Orphaned sync (set without immediate reset) recovers
  - Buffer lines match expected content after sync
  - Rapid sync on/off toggling
- [x] Verified manually with Claude Code running inside `:terminal` — permission prompts and tool output render without garbling
- [x] Existing terminal tests unaffected

## References

- [Synchronized Output spec](https://gist.github.com/christianparpart/d8a62cc1ab659194337d73e399004036)
- PR #25871 — TUI-side synchronized output support (`'termsync'`)
- [claude-code #20436](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/20436) — Bug report for garbled rendering in neovim's `:terminal`

comment_data.body:
in case it helps test things more directly, there is also `test/unit/vterm_spec.lua` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/8499af1119f0f96b4fd57ef9099ce5a2503bc952/test/unit/vterm_spec.lua

You can [run the test](https://neovim.io/doc/user/dev_test.html#dev-filter-test) like this:

    make unittest TEST_FILE=test/unit/vterm_spec.lua

+

comment_data.body:
Thanks — added unit tests in `vterm_spec.lua` covering `settermprop` callbacks and DECRQM query/response for mode 2026 (commit bf15adc).

Since the initial push, I also added a second commit (`fix(terminal): harden sync output redraw gating`) that addresses three additional redraw paths that bypassed the `invalidate_terminal()` gate:

1. **`term_sb_push` / `term_sb_pop`** — these scrollback handlers directly called `set_put(ptr_t, &invalidated_terminals, term)`, bypassing the synchronized_output check. Now gated.
2. **`refresh_timer_cb`** — could fire mid-sync and flush partial screen state. Now skips terminals in synchronized output mode.
3. **Sync-end flush timing** — instead of relying on the 10ms `REFRESH_DELAY` timer after sync ends (which left a window for stale repaints), sync-end now schedules a zero-delay full-screen refresh via a `sync_flush_pending` flag checked in `terminal_receive()`.

Happy to pull these out into a separate PR if you prefer — let me know your preference.

comment_data.body: I don't think the timer should be used here. If one terminal uses synchronized output very frequently (while others don't), this will cause other invalidated terminals to refresh at the same frequency. I think it's better to schedule a `refresh_terminal()` for only this terminal on the main loop.
There should be a `screen:expect_unchanged()` after this line. Otherwise this test is no different than the previous ones.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,165 @@
+local t = require('test.testutil')
+local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
+local Screen = require('test.functional.ui.screen')
+
+local clear = n.clear
+local api = n.api
+local eq = t.eq
+local poke_eventloop = n.poke_eventloop
+local assert_alive = n.assert_alive
+
+local CSI = '\027['
+
+describe(':terminal synchronized output (mode 2026)', function()
+  local screen, chan, buf
+
+  before_each(function()
+    clear()
+    screen = Screen.new(50, 7)
+    buf = api.nvim_create_buf(true, true)
+    chan = api.nvim_open_term(buf, {})
+    api.nvim_win_set_buf(0, buf)
+  end)
+
+  it('renders content sent inside a synchronized update', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'synced line 1\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'synced line 2\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^synced line 1                                     |
+      synced line 2                                     |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('renders all lines from a synchronized update', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    for i = 1, 5 do
+      api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'line ' .. i .. '\r\n')
+    end
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^line 1                                            |
+      line 2                                            |
+      line 3                                            |
+      line 4                                            |
+      line 5                                            |
+                                                        |
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('handles multiple synchronized update cycles', function()
+    -- First cycle.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'cycle 1\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^cycle 1                                           |
+                                                        |*5
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- Second cycle.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'cycle 2\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^cycle 1                                           |
+      cycle 2                                           |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('works with content before and after sync', function()
+    -- Unsynchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'before\r\n')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+                                                        |*5
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- Synchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'during\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+      during                                            |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- More unsynchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'after\r\n')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+      during                                            |
+      after                                             |
+                                                        |*3
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('does not crash when mode 2026 is set and queried', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    assert_alive()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    assert_alive()
+  end)
+
+  it('handles sync mode set without reset', function()
+    -- Begin sync but send reset later — terminal should recover.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'orphaned\r\n')

comment_data.body: This test can be merged into previous tests.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,165 @@
+local t = require('test.testutil')
+local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
+local Screen = require('test.functional.ui.screen')
+
+local clear = n.clear
+local api = n.api
+local eq = t.eq
+local poke_eventloop = n.poke_eventloop
+local assert_alive = n.assert_alive
+
+local CSI = '\027['
+
+describe(':terminal synchronized output (mode 2026)', function()
+  local screen, chan, buf
+
+  before_each(function()
+    clear()
+    screen = Screen.new(50, 7)
+    buf = api.nvim_create_buf(true, true)
+    chan = api.nvim_open_term(buf, {})
+    api.nvim_win_set_buf(0, buf)
+  end)
+
+  it('renders content sent inside a synchronized update', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'synced line 1\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'synced line 2\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^synced line 1                                     |
+      synced line 2                                     |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('renders all lines from a synchronized update', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    for i = 1, 5 do
+      api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'line ' .. i .. '\r\n')
+    end
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^line 1                                            |
+      line 2                                            |
+      line 3                                            |
+      line 4                                            |
+      line 5                                            |
+                                                        |
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('handles multiple synchronized update cycles', function()
+    -- First cycle.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'cycle 1\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^cycle 1                                           |
+                                                        |*5
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- Second cycle.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'cycle 2\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^cycle 1                                           |
+      cycle 2                                           |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('works with content before and after sync', function()
+    -- Unsynchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'before\r\n')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+                                                        |*5
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- Synchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'during\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+      during                                            |
+                                                        |*4
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    -- More unsynchronized content.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'after\r\n')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^before                                            |
+      during                                            |
+      after                                             |
+                                                        |*3
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+  end)
+
+  it('does not crash when mode 2026 is set and queried', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    assert_alive()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    assert_alive()
+  end)
+
+  it('handles sync mode set without reset', function()
+    -- Begin sync but send reset later — terminal should recover.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'orphaned\r\n')
+    -- Explicitly end the sync.
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^orphaned                                          |
+                                                        |*5
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+    assert_alive()
+  end)
+
+  it('buffer lines are correct after synchronized update', function()
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026h')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, 'aaa\r\nbbb\r\nccc\r\n')
+    api.nvim_chan_send(chan, CSI .. '?2026l')
+    -- Wait for the screen to update before checking buffer lines.
+    screen:expect([[
+      ^aaa                                               |
+      bbb                                               |
+      ccc                                               |
+                                                        |*3
+                                                        |
+    ]])
+
+    local lines = api.nvim_buf_get_lines(buf, 0, -1, true)
+    eq('aaa', lines[1])
+    eq('bbb', lines[2])
+    eq('ccc', lines[3])
+  end)

comment_data.body: Good point — changed to `multiqueue_put(main_loop.events, on_sync_flush, buf_handle)` which schedules a callback that refreshes only the specific terminal. (f864a67)
Added — the test now establishes a visible baseline, enters sync, sends content, and calls `screen:expect_unchanged()` to verify the screen doesn't update during sync. (f864a67)

comment_data.body: Merged the buffer-lines assertions into the "renders all lines" test and removed the standalone test. (f864a67)

+  end)

comment_regular_data.body:
Apologies for pushing additional functional changes after the initial review — I should have had these hardened before opening the PR. Here's a summary of what changed and why.

### What changed since the initial review:

**Commit 2** (`fix(terminal): harden sync output redraw gating`):

Testing with real-world TUI applications revealed three additional redraw paths that bypassed the `invalidate_terminal()` gate:

1. **`term_sb_push` / `term_sb_pop`** — directly called `set_put(ptr_t, &invalidated_terminals, term)`, bypassing the sync check
2. **`refresh_timer_cb`** — could fire mid-sync and flush partial screen state
3. **Sync-end flush timing** — the 10ms `REFRESH_DELAY` left a window for stale repaints. Now uses a zero-delay full-screen refresh via `sync_flush_pending` in `terminal_receive()`

Also added the `news.txt` changelog entry.

**Commit 3** (`test(terminal): add vterm unit tests for mode 2026`):

Per @justinmk's suggestion — added unit-level tests in `vterm_spec.lua` covering `settermprop` callbacks and DECRQM query/response for mode 2026.

### CI status

All checks pass except `macos intel clang functionaltest`, which fails on an existing test (`tui_spec.lua:3316 — FocusGained/FocusLost in hit-enter prompt`). This test passes on master and our changes don't touch any focus-related code, so this looks like a timing flake. Could a maintainer re-run that job to confirm?

### Known remaining issue

There is a residual display-layer rendering artifact that this PR does **not** fully resolve: in some cases, duplicate/stale rows appear briefly at the bottom of terminal windows after a topline adjustment followed by a small screen update. Through debug logging I confirmed the buffer content is correct after every sync flush — the issue appears to be in neovim's screen repaint pipeline (`set_topline` → `UPD_VALID` allowing row reuse when the visible window shifts). This seems to be a pre-existing terminal rendering issue not introduced by these changes, and I'm continuing to investigate it separately.

Happy to squash commits or make any other adjustments. Would appreciate a re-review when you get a chance — thank you!
